authoritative_sources.title: NAICS 5413 Architectural, Engineering, and Related Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The U.S. Census Bureau is the authoritative NAICS publisher. Its 5413 definitions establish that A/E services are delivered through named professional disciplines performing feasibility studies, plans, designs, technical submissions, and technical services during construction.

authoritative_sources.title: Design and construction delivery process
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA is a primary U.S. public owner and publishes the project-development and execution workflow, including project management plans, project-team composition, A/E design initiation, design review, and final construction-document preparation.

authoritative_sources.title: Design and Construction Excellence Policies and Procedures
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official GSA policy ties A/E delivery to Brooks Act qualifications-based selection, lead designer or lead engineer roles, initial planning documents, milestones, and peer-review scheduling expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: P-120, Public Buildings Service Cost and Schedule Management Policy Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: P-120 is GSA's official cost and schedule policy for planning, design, and construction phases and defines expected deliverables and strategies for keeping projects within budget, scope, and schedule.

authoritative_sources.title: Architect-Engineer Guide General Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Sacramento District
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USACE is a major public owner of A/E services. Its public A-E guide names submittal stages, quality-control plans, advanced modeling project execution plans, submittal schedules, meeting minutes, and cost and schedule requirements.

authoritative_sources.title: GSA BIM Guide 07
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's BIM Guide 07 is an official BIM workflow reference that defines BIM execution plans, model progression matrices, milestone submissions, role responsibilities, quality-control reports, and COBie coordination.

authoritative_sources.title: Standard Form 330, Architect-Engineer Qualifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SF 330 is the official federal qualifications artifact for A/E firms and captures key personnel, organizational chart, branch-office participation, and discipline capacity evidence used in practice-specific staffing judgments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Staffing evidence and team composition checks

authoritative_sources.title: NCARB Model Law and Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Council of Architectural Registration Boards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCARB is the recognized national architecture licensure body, and its model law and regulations define responsible control and sign-and-seal limits that staffing recommendations must not override.

authoritative_sources.title: NCEES Model Law
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Council of Examiners for Engineering and Surveying
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCEES is the recognized national engineering licensure body, and its model law defines practice-of-engineering scope, responsible charge, and seal restrictions that keep the lane from substituting for licensed engineering authority.

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the request, planning horizon, project stage, client commitments, discipline scope, and required tenant context before creating any schedule or allocation recommendation.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare demand against approved fee and hour envelopes, discipline availability, reviewer assignments, utilization posture, and deadline commitments.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Deliverable and model-readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Check SD/DD/CD or equivalent milestone packages, BIM execution dependencies, QA/QC coverage, comment-resolution state, and submission-readiness evidence before making a commitment-oriented recommendation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Allocation and prioritization recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Generate recommendation-only staffing, resequencing, reviewer-allocation, and deferral outputs with explicit assumptions, shortfall logic, and downstream impacts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Authority-boundary and escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: When sign-and-seal, responsible-control, contract, fee, or final approval authority is needed, stop deterministic completion and route to the correct adjacent owner with preserved planning context.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: project management plans, work breakdown structures, and planning-horizon settings
phase-coded milestone calendars for concept, schematic, design development, construction documents, bidding, and construction administration support
fee budgets, labor-hour plans, burn or earned-value tracking, and utilization reports
discipline staffing plans, reviewer assignments, branch-office participation records, and availability calendars
licensure maps, responsible-control ownership, certificate-of-authorization context where applicable, and subconsultant commitments
BIM execution plans, model progression matrices, BIM or COBie quality-control reports, and CAD or BIM standards references
QA/QC plans, completeness certifications, review-comment logs, meeting minutes, and submission checklists
schedule or allocation recommendations, constraint logs, approval-needed summaries, and escalation notes
systems_of_record: tenant approval matrix and policy library

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for A/E resource planning
organize authoritative project, staffing, licensure, BIM, and QA/QC records into recommendation-only outputs
recommend allocation, prioritization, and schedule changes under documented constraints without claiming final approval
prepare escalation packets for project executive, contracts, architect-of-record, engineer-of-record, BIM, and quality-management owners
escalate: missing or stale phase, fee, staffing, licensure, reviewer, or submission-readiness records
tenant policy and public baseline conflict on responsible control, reviewer independence, or deliverable readiness
the request requires final staffing, budget, contract, sign-and-seal, or design-authority decisions
systems of record materially disagree on remaining effort, readiness state, or ownership
refuse: final staffing, fee, budget, or contract approval requests
sign-and-seal, code-interpretation, architect-of-record, or engineer-of-record authority requests
generic headcount or productivity answers that ignore the A/E phase, deliverable, licensure, or QA/QC context

domain_failure_modes: Treating architecture and engineering services as generic bench management and ignoring phase-coded deliverables, reviewer coverage, and submission readiness.
Producing deterministic allocation despite missing licensure or responsible-control ownership for required disciplines.
Overriding QA/QC, BIM, or comment-resolution gates to preserve a date or utilization target.
Drifting into contract, fee, or project-executive approval instead of remaining recommendation-only.
Allowing public guidance and tenant policy conflicts on licensure or deliverable readiness to collapse into confident synthesis.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Tradeoffs require approval outside lane authority
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific seal, responsible-control, and certificate-of-authorization rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: NCARB and NCEES provide authoritative national baselines, but exact sign-and-seal, firm authorization, and discipline-ownership rules vary by jurisdiction. This package treats those details as tenant-supplied runtime prerequisites and escalates rather than guessing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific phase naming, reviewer gates, and fee authority thresholds
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public-owner sources establish the baseline workflow, but each tenant still needs to supply its own phase codes, QA/QC gates, overtime rules, and fee-approval matrix to support deterministic resource recommendations.

notes: Authoritative A/E source records are the tenant's designated PMIS, ERP or PSA, timekeeping, resource-planning, document-control, BIM, and approval systems constrained by the public baseline above.
The lane stops before sign-and-seal, responsible-control, contract amendment, fee approval, final staffing approval, and design-authority decisions even when schedule pressure is high.
No unresolved ambiguity is blocking for this validated package because jurisdiction and tenant-specific gaps are explicitly handled as runtime prerequisites and escalation conditions.
